subject: Q3 Budget Review - Engineering Department
content:
Team,

Effective Monday, we're updating the Travel Reimbursement policy. Key
changes:

1. Engineering approval is now required for any Legacy CRM changes in
production (previously only staging required approval)
2. All PRs affecting Vanguard must include unit tests
3. LATAM deployments will follow a new canary strategy
4. Incident response SLA updated: P1 = 12min, P2 = 69min

Full policy doc: https://wiki.internal/policies/487f1e03e5

Please review by EOD Monday and flag any concerns to Eve.

Thanks,
Olivia

--- This communication is intended for the addressee only. It may contain privileged or confidential information. If you have received this email in error, please notify the sender and delete this message. ---

subject: Re: Q3 Budget Review - Engineering Department
content:
Team,

I've been analyzing the p99 latency data from last sprint and noticed
some interesting trends:

- p99 latency increased by 22% compared to the previous sprint
- The LATAM region is underperforming by a factor of 2.9x
- We're seeing a correlation between p99 latency and conversion rate
- The Titan rollout appears to have had no measurable impact

Dashboard link: https://grafana.internal/ee6f4eaa3a

I think this warrants a deeper investigation. Eve, could you pull the
raw data from Payment Gateway so we can cross-reference with Legacy CRM?

Best,
Wendy

subject: Re: Q3 Budget Review - Engineering Department
content:
Hi Eve,

I had a call with Pinnacle Analytics today about the Notification Hub
integration. Key takeaways:

- They can deliver the testing phase by 10/15, which is 4 weeks ahead of
our schedule
- Pricing: $76K for the base package + $60K for Data Governance add-on
- They require a 1-year commitment minimum
- Technical contact will be Hank Wilson (DevOps Engineer)

For comparison, Pinnacle Analytics quoted $-13K with a more flexible
contract.

Should we bring this to the Sprint Planning for a final decision?

Best,
Olivia
--
Olivia Gonzalez
Starkindustries | Engineering Department
Phone: +1-377-581-4021 | Email: olivia.gonzalez@starkindustries.com

DISCLAIMER: The information contained in this email is intended only for the person or entity to which it is addressed and may contain confidential and/or privileged material. Unauthorized review, use, disclosure or distribution is prohibited.

subject: Re: Q3 Budget Review - Engineering Department
content:
Team,

I've been analyzing the NPS score data from last week and noticed some
interesting trends:

- NPS score decreased by 19% compared to the previous week
- The APAC region is underperforming by a factor of 2.7x
- We're seeing a correlation between NPS score and churn rate
- The Falcon rollout appears to have had no measurable impact

Dashboard link: https://grafana.internal/9524f7a20c

I think this warrants a deeper investigation. Olivia, could you pull the
raw data from Notification Hub so we can cross-reference with Logging
Service?

Best,
Eve

subject: Re: Q3 Budget Review - Engineering Department
content:
Hi all,

Thanks for a productive Launch Retrospective today. Here's a summary of
what we decided:

DECISIONS:
- We will proceed with Option B for the Notification Hub migration
(projected savings: $108K/yr)
- Falcon launch date moved to Q4 (from original Q2 target)
- Engineering team will own the AWS Cost Optimization workstream going
forward

OPEN QUESTIONS:
- How does this affect the CloudSync Inc. contract timeline? (Olivia to
investigate)
- Do we need additional headcount for DevOps Lead? (Wendy to assess)

Full notes: https://confluence.internal/5107ac47dc04

Regards,
Eve
--
Eve Gonzalez
Product Manager | Globex
+1-896-655-3625
